title: Islamic Republic of Iran Poverty Assessment (World Bank, November 2023) — national, rural/urban, and regional/provincial poverty trends 2011-2020
source_org: World Bank, Poverty Global Practice, Middle East & North Africa

files.name: iran-poverty-assessment-november-2023.pdf
files.notes: Full report, led by Erwin W. Knippenberg (Economist, TTL) et al. Sections used: Rural-Urban Divide (pp.27-28), Increasing Regional Disparities (pp.30-31), Profile of the Poor (pp.32+). Based on Iran's own annual Household Income and Expenditure Survey (HIES) 2011-2020, province-stratified.

files.name: figure24-poverty-rate-by-region-2011-2020.png
files.notes: Rendered page 31 (pdftoppm -r 150) containing Figure 24: precise labeled bar chart, poverty headcount % by 8 macro-region (Central/Tehran Metro/Northwest/Zagros/Caspian/Persian Gulf/Northeast/Southeast) + national, 2011 vs 2020 -- values manually transcribed into the processed CSV (see data/processed/worldbank_poverty_equity/), cross-checked twice against the image, all figures have exact printed data labels (no eyeballing needed for this figure)

files.name: figure25-26-poverty-rate-by-province-map-and-correlation.png
files.notes: Rendered page 32 containing Figure 25 (choropleth map of all 31 provinces by poverty-rate BUCKET [0-15/15-30/30-45/45-65/65-80%], 2011 vs 2020 -- NOT individually labeled with exact per-province percentages, so NOT transcribed into a precise CSV to avoid fabricating precision the source doesn't provide; two provinces ARE given exact narrative figures in the body text: West Azerbaijan 13.6%->44% [2011->2020, largest increase nationally] and Bushehr declining over the same period [exact 2020 figure not stated]) and Figure 26 (scatter: change in provincial poverty rate vs. % of labor force in agriculture and % with secondary schooling -- strong positive correlation with agricultural-employment share, strong negative with secondary-school attainment, visible in the plot but not given as an exact per-province data table)

frequency: point-in-time assessment covering 2 survey years (2011, 2020) plus annual HIES trend discussion 2011-2020
units: poverty headcount ratio, percent of population (national poverty line, Iran's own HIES-based line per World Bank methodology)
license_terms: World Bank Documents & Reports — open access, CC BY 3.0 IGO typical for WB reports
notes: DIRECTLY ANSWERS project brief bucket 1 (provincial poverty rates) with a genuine, recent, high-quality primary-derived source (Iran's own annual HIES, the same survey underlying the IHEIS microdata already tracked in data/raw/iheis-microdata-metadata/) -- this is a different angle from the existing GDP-by-province and SCI Jaygah-e-Ostanha compendium (Round 29): those give GDP/economic-structure by province, this gives POVERTY specifically with a clean before/after decade comparison. Key findings recorded: national poverty rose from 20%->28% of population 2011-2020; Southeast region rose 43%->52% (highest); Northwest region nearly doubled 16%->36% wskutek drought/agriculture-dependence; Tehran Metro barely moved 14%->16% (most resilient); rural poverty (2020) is roughly double urban; West Azerbaijan province specifically more than tripled (13.6%->44%, largest single-province increase); Bushehr province is one of the few where poverty FELL over the decade (consistent with its top-5 GDP-share provincial-GDP finding in Round 29 -- oil/gas revenue base). Strong correlation identified between rising provincial poverty and (a) higher agricultural-employment share and (b) lower secondary-school attainment. A companion Box 5 in the report quantifies drought's macro channel: a modeled 20% water-supply reduction could cut Iran's GDP by up to 7.2% vs 2016 levels and reduce agricultural labor demand by up to 4.6% (Taheripour et al. 2020, cited in the WB report) -- relevant supporting context for the drought-economic-cost angle in this round's disaster bucket too.

failures.reason: Islamic Republic of Iran Poverty and Equity Brief (October 2024, shorter companion brief) landing page returned no visible PDF link via WebFetch (JS-rendered portal); the November 2023 full Poverty Assessment PDF (found via direct documents1.worldbank.org search result) supplied everything needed and more, so the Oct 2024 brief was not pursued further
